resume_profile.name: ALEX TAN MING WEI
resume_profile.summary: Systems-oriented RTIS graduate with hands-on experience building real-time simulation systems, REST APIs, and containerised infrastructure tools.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Real-Time Interactive Simulation (RTIS)

projects.title: Distributed Task Scheduler
bullets: Designed a Python-based distributed task scheduler using Redis and Docker that reduced job processing time by 40%, handling 500+ concurrent tasks.
Implemented RESTful APIs with FastAPI and deployed on AWS ECS using Terraform, achieving 99.9% uptime over a 3-month period.
Automated CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions, cutting deployment time from 30 minutes to under 5 minutes.

projects.title: Real-Time Physics Engine
bullets: Built a real-time application loop in C++ with Vulkan rendering that sustained 60 fps on 500+ dynamic objects simultaneously.
Implemented spatial algorithms (AABB broad-phase, SAT narrow-phase) in C++, reducing collision computation time by 35%.
Deployed cross-platform on Windows and Linux using CMake.

projects.title: Linux Monitoring Dashboard
bullets: Developed a Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack with Bash automation scripts that detected system anomalies 3x faster than manual log review.
Containerised the stack with Docker Compose; integrated Kubernetes Helm charts for scaling across 10-node clusters.

experience.title: Systems Engineering Intern
bullets: Automated infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and Ansible across AWS GCP environments, reducing manual setup time by 60%.
Wrote Python scripts to parse and validate Kubernetes pod logs, enabling the team to resolve incidents 50% faster.

required_skills: Proficiency in at least one systems programming language: Python, Go, or C++
Experience with Linux operating systems and shell scripting (Bash)
Understanding of networking fundamentals: TCP/IP, DNS, load balancing
Experience with at least one cloud platform: AWS, GCP, or Azure
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Good written and verbal communication in English
preferred_skills: Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or equivalent)
Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Pulumi)
Knowledge of distributed systems and consensus algorithms
Exposure to financial systems or trading platforms
responsibilities: Design and implement RESTful APIs and microservices using Python and Go
Automate infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and Ansible
Monitor system health using Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack
Participate in on-call rotation and incident response
Collaborate with software engineers, data engineers, and QA teams

missing.why_it_matters: The JD explicitly requires understanding of networking fundamentals, including load balancing, for infrastructure design.
The JD lists Azure as a preferred cloud platform; adding it expands the candidate's cloud experience.
Exposure to financial systems is preferred; mentioning relevant project context can address this requirement.
The JD requires good communication skills; this should be explicitly stated or demonstrated in the experience section.

ats_red_flags.evidence: Project dates use '?' (e.g., Distributed Task Scheduler (Aug 2025 ? Dec 2025)). While this is a formatting choice in the input, it introduces slight ambiguity for strict ATS parsing of timeline data.

background_fit.candidate_background_summary: The candidate is a Systems-oriented graduate with hands-on experience building real-time simulation systems, REST APIs, and containerized infrastructure tools.
background_fit.role_requirements_summary: The role requires proficiency in systems programming (Python/Go/C++), Linux skills, cloud experience, Infrastructure as Code, and familiarity with distributed systems and observability tools.
background_fit.alignment_commentary: The candidate demonstrates strong alignment by possessing the required languages (Python, C++, Go, Bash) and extensive practical experience with core infrastructure technologies such as Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, Ansible, AWS, GCP, Prometheus, and Grafana. The project work directly addresses preferred skills like CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and distributed systems.

summary:
*   The résumé failed the ATS threshold with a low overall score of 44, indicating that while core technical skills are present, optimization is needed to pass automated screening.
*   Critical gaps exist
